name: San José
tagline: Pura Vida Living in Central America's Most Stable Democracy
description: San José is the gateway to Costa Rica's legendary Pura Vida lifestyle — a sprawling capital surrounded by volcanoes, cloud forests, and some of the planet's richest biodiversity. While not a beach town itself, San José offers excellent healthcare, a stable democratic government, established expat infrastructure, and the popular Pensionado retirement visa. The Central Valley's eternal spring climate and proximity to both Pacific and Caribbean coasts make it a practical, comfortable base for FIRE retirees seeking tropical living with First World services.

quickFacts.visa: Pensionado visa for retirees with $1,000+/month guaranteed income. Rentista visa for those with $2,500+/month. Permanent residency after 3 years.
quickFacts.timezone: UTC-6 (Central Standard Time, no daylight saving)
quickFacts.language: Spanish (English spoken in expat areas and medical facilities)
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderate — petty theft common in downtown areas, safer in suburbs like Escazú and Santa Ana; avoid walking alone at night in central San José
quickFacts.nearestAirport: Juan Santamaría International Airport (SJO) — 20 min from city center
quickFacts.climate: Tropical highland with eternal spring weather (18-26°C year-round). Dry season Dec-Apr, rainy season May-Nov with afternoon showers.
quickFacts.currency: Costa Rican Colón (CRC) / US Dollar widely accepted

housing.description: Comfortable 1-bedroom apartment in Escazú or Sabana area with security, close to shops and restaurants

dining.description: Daily casados at local sodas, occasional dining out at neighborhood restaurants, strong coffee culture at local cafés

healthcare.description: CAJA public healthcare enrollment plus affordable private insurance supplement for faster specialist access at CIMA or Clínica Bíblica

transportation.description: Local bus system, occasional Uber or taxi rides around the Central Valley

entertainment.description: Weekend trips to nearby volcanoes and cloud forests, farmers markets, National Theater performances, neighborhood parks

1M.lifestyle: A one-bedroom apartment in Escazú or the Sabana area with security gives you a base in a safe suburb. Casados at local sodas cost a few dollars, the CAJA public healthcare system provides basic coverage, and weekend trips to nearby volcanoes or cloud forests are cheap by bus or shared ride. The budget is comfortable but modest — you cook at home regularly, rely on public transit, and keep entertainment low-cost.

housing.description: Modern 2-bedroom apartment or townhouse in Escazú or Santa Ana with mountain views, pool, and gated security

dining.description: Regular dining at Escazú's international restaurants, weekend brunches, craft beer scene, farm-to-table Tico cuisine

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private insurance covering CIMA Hospital, dental, vision, and prescription medications with minimal wait times

transportation.description: Leased compact car for Central Valley exploration, Uber for evenings out, weekend drives to the coast

entertainment.description: Zip-lining and rafting weekends, Pacific coast beach trips, Arenal hot springs, expat social clubs, farmers market tours

2M.lifestyle: A modern two-bedroom in Escazú or Santa Ana with mountain views, pool, and gated security gives you a comfortable base. A leased car unlocks both coasts within three hours, you dine at international restaurants several times a week, and comprehensive private insurance at CIMA Hospital provides excellent healthcare. The expat community is active with hiking groups, dinner clubs, and volunteer projects.

housing.description: Luxury 3-bedroom house in Santa Ana or Escazú hills with private garden, pool, and sweeping Central Valley views

dining.description: Fine dining at San José's best restaurants, personal chef occasionally, curated coffee experiences at premium fincas, weekend coastal seafood trips

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance with international coverage, concierge medical services at CIMA and Clínica Bíblica, wellness retreats

transportation.description: Owned SUV for mountain and coastal drives, Uber for city outings, domestic flights to Guanacaste and Osa Peninsula

entertainment.description: Premium eco-lodge weekends, private nature tours, National Theater season tickets, country club membership, surfing lessons on both coasts

3M.lifestyle: A house in the Santa Ana or Escazú hills with a private garden, pool, and Central Valley views anchors daily life. You own an SUV for coastal and mountain drives, dine at the city's best restaurants regularly, and carry premium international health insurance. Weekend trips to eco-lodges in Arenal, surfing in Nosara, or the Osa Peninsula are comfortably in budget, and domestic flights save time on longer trips.

housing.description: Luxury estate in the Escazú hills or Santa Ana with private pool, tropical gardens, guest house, and panoramic valley views

dining.description: Private chef specializing in fusion Tico-international cuisine, fine dining as routine, curated Costa Rican coffee collection, premium wine cellar

healthcare.description: International concierge medicine, private physician, wellness retreats at premium jungle spas, anti-aging protocols

transportation.description: Luxury SUV, private driver available, domestic charter flights to beach destinations, regular private transfers

entertainment.description: Private nature reserves tours, deep-sea fishing charters, exclusive eco-lodge access, art collecting from Costa Rican artists, philanthropy

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, gardener for estate grounds, pool maintenance, personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: Private Spanish tutor, personal trainer, bespoke eco-adventure planning, conservation project involvement, premium country club membership

5M.lifestyle: A large estate in the Escazú hills with a pool, tropical gardens, and guest house provides generous space. A housekeeper manages the home, a cook comes several days a week, and a driver handles transportation. You dine at the best restaurants freely, fly business class for international trips, and take charter flights to remote beach or rainforest destinations. Premium healthcare and a nice car round out a comfortable, well-staffed daily life.

housing.description: Ultra-luxury compound in the Central Valley hills with multiple residences, infinity pool, helipad, and a secondary beachfront property on the Pacific coast

dining.description: Full-time private chef team, rare coffee and cacao sourcing, legendary dinner parties with international guests, farm-to-table experiences at your own organic garden

healthcare.description: Global VIP medical coverage, dedicated wellness team, private jungle spa facility, fly-anywhere specialist access, longevity programs

transportation.description: Chauffeured luxury fleet, private helicopter for coast-to-coast travel, yacht charter on the Pacific, private jet for international trips

entertainment.description: Major conservation patronage, private wildlife reserves, art foundation, hosting international environmental summits, deep-sea sportfishing yacht

domesticHelp.description: Full estate staff: butler, chef team, housekeepers, groundskeepers, driver, personal assistant, estate manager, security

luxuryExtras.description: Private nature reserve ownership, conservation foundation, organic farm development, bespoke Central American travel experiences, rare coffee estate investment

10M.lifestyle: A large compound in the Central Valley hills plus a secondary beachfront property on the Pacific coast gives you two distinct homes. A cook, housekeeper, groundskeeping staff, and driver manage daily logistics. You fly first class internationally, dine without any budget constraints, and have the resources to support conservation or community projects. This budget significantly exceeds Costa Rica's cost ceiling — substantial margin goes to savings, additional travel, or philanthropic work.
